hard.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - Alexa to share the updates with ANA that we put together for McAfee RE: SMS/whatsapp/etc. to showcase our consistent innovation vs SAP. Hoping this action generates a response one way or another on the outcome of the SAP beta
hard.ais_why: The 1/21/2026 Allstate on-site captures a direct buyer-driven AI action: Zycus to schedule two reference calls with customers actively using Zycus AI capabilities. Active ANA POC track documented across 2/25, 3/2 and 4/13/2026. The $1.5M deal is replacing SAP Ariba, with Merlin in the product set.

north_star_verdict.headline: A live, well-regarded Shortlisted deal that has stalled: the SAP Ariba beta/workshop is still running with no decision reported, the ANA POC has been offered but never resourced, and the buyer has been quiet since the 22 May 2026 email with Zycus follow-ups on 08 Jun and the 29 Jun McAfee-innovation nudge unanswered. The relationship is warm and the AI story lands, but momentum has thinned and the 31 Jul close date cannot hold.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 25 (close 2026-07-31, today 2026-07-06). Stage: Shortlisted (MID), qualified 06 Mar 2024. Last two-way buyer contact: 22 May 2026 email (Gaurav: 'Let me connect with our leadership'); rep-side outreach 20 May, 08 Jun and the 29 Jun ANA/McAfee nudge with no buyer reply since. Remaining gates from Shortlisted: run the ANA POC (3-4 wks), deliver 2 AI reference calls, lock updated commercials (2-3 wks), activate Economic Buyer Casey McDowell (never engaged), then legal + MSA (6-8 wks). That is 90-120 days of enterprise motion against 25 days: the date will slip. Close date already pushed 30 Jun -> 31 Jul on 16 Jun 2026 (its third slip after 31 Mar -> 31 May -> 30 Jun).
evidence: SAP Ariba beta/workshop still running as of 26 May 2026 per Next_Step__c ('they are currently still doing a SAP Ariba workshop/beta program'), with no closure reported through the 16 Jun entry ('no update on the beta or evaluation from gaurav')

ANA POC offered 25 Feb 2026 and framework/success-criteria discussed 02 Mar 2026, but the buyer's technology team was still 'planning for resources allocation' as of the 21 May 2026 email; the POC has never been executed
Economic Buyer Casey McDowell (VP Global Enterprise Procurement) exec session planned since 04 Feb 2026 and reiterated 18 Feb, never held per Next_Step__c through 29 Jun 2026
Two AI-focused reference calls promised at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite ('need to set up different references ASAP') still pending per the 28 Apr Next_Step__c
Updated commercial proposal outstanding since 13 Apr / 28 Apr 2026 ('fu with guarav on all pending items - POC, Reference, and updated proposal')
Last buyer reply 22 May 2026; Zycus outreach 20 May, 08 Jun and the 29 Jun ANA/McAfee nudge unanswered, indicating engagement is thinning, not dark (last activity 08 Jun 2026)

metrics.narrative: The pain is a full SAP Ariba lift-and-shift to a modern, AI-led S2P suite, and the compelling event is concrete: the Ariba contract renews on a 12-month clock stated at the 21 Jan 2026 onsite ('resigning ariba 12 months expires in 12 months'), forcing a decision by roughly Q1 2027. The buyer articulated qualitative value themes on that call, AI-driven autonomy (Shiv cited ANA autonomous negotiation delivering 3-4% incremental savings at Tata Play), intake automation, and bandwidth freed in a 40-person sourcing team (Mohit Bothra probed 'can we say we created 5 FTE bandwidth'). However, no quantified business case has been built for Allstate: no target savings figure, no cycle-time or headcount baseline, no TCO comparison against an Ariba renewal. Quantifying that value case is the move, because without it the Economic Buyer cannot justify a rip-and-replace to the CFO.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is mapped but never engaged: Casey McDowell (VP - Global Enterprise Procurement) is the named Executive Sponsor on the OpportunityContactRole and the target of an exec session Karson has been trying to set since 04 Feb 2026 ('working on setting up executive session with Casey in Chicago'), reiterated 18 Feb, but it has never been held. Cheryl Harris (SVP, Procurement) sits above him and is also an Executive Sponsor / Evaluator on the roster but is likewise unengaged. Nags (Nage) leads global source-to-pay operations and reports into that leadership. Budget authority therefore clearly resides with Casey and Cheryl, but neither has appeared on a call, so sponsorship is unproven. Landing the Casey session and converting it to written sponsorship is the gating move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Casey McDowell Executive Sponsor; Cheryl Harris Executive Sponsor)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well-articulated from the 21 Jan 2026 onsite: (1) proven, referenceable AI adoption, the buyer explicitly rejected the three earlier lift-and-shift references as not demonstrating AI use ('we could not get a good hang or a good sense of their AI use cases') and demanded AI-adopter references instead; (2) SAP-to-Zycus migration confidence, they asked for a 'ready-to-ready checklist' and data-migration approach for moving off SAP; (3) complex approval-workflow flexibility (hierarchy plus panel plus finance plus tech routing) which Mohit Bothra stress-tested at length; (4) supplier data structuring and partitioning between sourcing and contracting ('that's a today's gap as well'); and (5) implementation timeline, the buyer pushed on the ~9-month S2C-plus-P2P rollout and the 12-month cycle. These are confirmed, buyer-stated criteria, not inferred.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, the buyer is running a structured, committee-led replacement evaluation: RFP presentations already completed and praised ('fabulous job from an RFP presentations point of view'), a large product/strategy onsite on 21 Jan 2026 with the product team plus Mohit Bothra (S2P ops and AI/automation lead) and Nage (global S2P ops), and a parallel SAP Ariba beta/workshop as the comparative track. Buyer stated at the onsite 'decision in next 3/4 months'. The next intended gates are the ANA POC and AI reference calls feeding an executive session with Casey McDowell and leadership. The approval chain (multiple approvals, procurement plus finance plus tech sign-off) is implied by the workflow discussion but not formally documented, and the process has stalled on the buyer's side allocating POC resources.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible, which is consistent with a Shortlisted, pre-POC deal. No MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement-channel detail has surfaced in any call, task or field. A deep legal bench IS on the account roster (Elliot Stultz, SVP and Deputy General Counsel; Travis Rogers, Senior Managing Counsel; Deb Sokol, Head of Privacy, Data and AI Legal), signalling that AI-governance and data-privacy review will matter when paper starts, but none is engaged today. The Ariba renewal (~Q1 2027) is the only forcing function on record. Visibility will come from running the POC and the exec session, which unlock the commercial and legal tracks; there is nothing to redline yet.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed operational pain is running procurement on an SAP Ariba stack the buyer is actively looking to replace, with specific friction the team named on 21 Jan 2026: complex approval-workflow uniqueness (hierarchy plus panel plus finance plus tech routing) that current tooling handles poorly, UI/adoption resistance in a compliance-heavy shop, tail and Maverick spend that goes un-negotiated today ('spend you're probably not even touching'), and supplier data structuring/partitioning gaps between RFP, contracting and invoicing ('that's a today's gap as well'). Mohit Bothra (S2P ops plus AI/automation stream owner) and Nage (global S2P ops) own this pain. Inferred pain is Ariba-renewal inertia if Zycus cannot prove differentiated AI value in time. The pain is real and well-articulated; only its dollar quantification is still missing.

champion.narrative: The working champion is Gaurav (Gaurav Rakesh Desai in the Clari email tasks), named across Next_Step__c and the email trail as the buyer contact driving the evaluation ('gaurav connecting w leadership on next steps with POC, commercials' on 26 May 2026). He facilitates access, coordinates the Casey exec-session setup, and shepherds the POC and references. His advocacy is genuine but his conversion power is unproven: across five months he has not delivered the executed POC, the AI reference calls, or the Casey/Cheryl session, and he has gone quiet since 22 May 2026 ('Let me connect with our leadership and take their inputs'). Hygiene gap: 'Gaurav' appears only in activities/Next_Step and does not map to a named OpportunityContactRole, so his exact identity is not formally recorded. The deal is effectively single-threaded on him; multi-threading to Mohit Bothra (Decision Maker) and directly to Casey McDowell is the way to de-risk.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 21 Jan, 28 Apr, 26 May, 08 Jun 2026
SF email tasks 21 May, 22 May 2026 (Gaurav Rakesh Desai)

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and the live threat: Allstate is on SAP today and is running a parallel Ariba beta/workshop as of 26 May 2026, so the real fight is Zycus displacement versus Ariba incumbency and renewal inertia, framed on 15 Dec 2025 as 'confirmed SAP VS Zycus'. Coupa and iValua sit in Competitors__c but have no recent buyer mention and are dormant. The wedge is AI velocity: the buyer explicitly praised Zycus's RFP presentations ('fabulous job', 'at par') and is pulling for AI-adopter references plus the ANA autonomous-negotiation and agentic-intake capabilities Ariba cannot match on the same timeline. Zycus wins by turning a scoped ANA POC and two credible AI references into proof that closes the buyer's stated AI-uncertainty before the Ariba beta concludes.

critical_signals.text: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and Allstate is still running a live Ariba beta/workshop, so the award is gated on that beta rather than on any Zycus gap.
At the 21 January onsite the buyer praised the RFP work as 'at par' and 'a fabulous job' but rejected the three lift-and-shift references and demanded proof of real AI adoption.
An updated proposal has been outstanding since mid-April with no ROI or TCO-vs-Ariba-renewal case built, so the budget owner has nothing to justify a rip-and-replace.
Economic buyer Casey McDowell (VP Global Enterprise Procurement) is mapped but has never been engaged despite an exec session being chased since early February.

deal_movement.summary: Reads from Next_Step_History__c, field history and the email trail: the deal is warm but stalled. No new buyer call this sweep; the only movement since the last read is rep-side (the 29 Jun ANA/McAfee-innovation nudge) plus the 16 Jun close-date push to 31 Jul. The buyer has not replied since the 22 May email. Verdict trajectory: holding steady at Slowing (marginally weaker as the buyer silence extends and the close date slips a third time), the single most likely blocker to close remaining the unresolved SAP Ariba beta and the unactivated Economic Buyer.

items.change: Close date pushed 30 Jun -> 31 Jul (third slip after 31 Mar -> 31 May -> 30 Jun)
Rep nudge: Alexa to share the McAfee ANA/innovation update with ANA to force a response on the SAP beta outcome
Follow-up email to Gaurav on where the beta sits and whether they are exploring vendors beyond Ariba; no reply
Buyer's last two-way reply: Gaurav to connect with leadership on how to proceed
Buyer stated they are still running a SAP Ariba workshop/beta program; Gaurav connecting with leadership on POC and commercials

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: it is the incumbent and Allstate is running a live Ariba beta/workshop as of 26 May 2026, so the award is gated on that beta plus internal alignment. Zycus is well-regarded ('at par', 'fabulous job') and differentiates on AI velocity (Merlin, ANA autonomous negotiation, agentic intake). Coupa and iValua are named in Competitors__c but dormant with no recent buyer mention. Fieldglass (with Beeline as an alternative) is the incumbent VMS on the adjacent contingent-labour track. Do-nothing / Ariba renewal is a real risk given incumbent inertia and the ~Q1 2027 contract expiry.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: break the buyer silence, activate the economic buyer, and convert the AI story into hard proof before the Ariba beta closes.
flags: Break the 22 May silence: get the SAP Ariba beta outcome in writing from Gaurav before assuming the deal is still live, then re-baseline the plan.
Activate the economic buyer: land the long-promised Casey McDowell (and Cheryl Harris) exec session, the deal cannot close without their sponsorship and it has slipped since February.
Convert AI positioning to proof: deliver the two AI-adopter reference calls and a scoped ANA POC to neutralise the buyer's stated AI-adoption uncertainty against SAP.

Developing and at risk while the SAP Ariba beta plays out. His identity is not mapped to a formal contact role, so multi-threading to Mohit Bothra and Casey McDowell is essential.

The gap is proof, not positioning: the references and POC that would convert interest to conviction are undelivered.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 8.0 / Strong, corroborated by the calls: the buyer is actively demanding AI-adopter references and probing agentic intake and autonomous negotiation, so the field is accurately rated, not over-stated.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Allstate is a well-regarded $1.5M SAP-Ariba-replacement where Zycus's AI story clearly lands, but the deal has stalled: the buyer is running a parallel SAP beta, has gone quiet since 22 May, and the economic buyer has never been engaged, so the 31 Jul date will not hold.

win_position.text: Shortlisted (MID) caps confidence in the low-mid range; the deal earns credit for strong AI fit and a genuine 'at par'/'fabulous job' buyer read on the RFP, but holds down because the two decisive gates (economic-buyer sponsorship and referenceable AI proof) are both unmet.

AI is a real differentiator here: on 21 Jan Mohit Bothra pulled Zycus to swap in AI-adopter references and engaged deeply on agentic intake and the ANA autonomous-negotiation demo, so the technical preference leans our way.
Allstate is still running a live SAP Ariba beta/workshop as of 26 May and the award is gated on that outcome, so an engaged incumbent, not a Zycus weakness, is the primary risk to the win.
Economic buyer Casey McDowell has never been engaged despite exec-session attempts since February, and with no quantified ROI/TCO case there is nothing for leadership to justify a rip-and-replace on.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has thinned: the buyer's last two-way reply was the 22 May email, and Zycus follow-ups on 08 Jun and the 29 Jun ANA/McAfee nudge went unanswered.
The close date has slipped three times (31 Mar to 31 May to 30 Jun to 31 Jul), and the ANA POC offered on 25 Feb was never resourced by the buyer's tech team.
The relationship is still warm rather than dark: Gaurav was actively coordinating leadership on POC and commercials as recently as 26 May before going quiet.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer invested a full product/strategy onsite on 21 Jan with the S2P ops and AI/automation lead present and stated a decision was expected 'in next 3/4 months'.
Commitment is soft on the commercial track: the updated proposal has sat unaddressed since mid-April and the buyer has not committed POC resources or an exec-session date.

deal_risk.text: Single-thread risk on Gaurav, who has gone quiet while an internal SAP Ariba beta plays out, with no direct line yet to the economic buyer.
Do-nothing / Ariba-renewal inertia is a live no-decision risk given the incumbent stack and the ~Q1 2027 renewal clock.

deal_risk.evidence: Live SAP Ariba beta plus unengaged economic buyer plus single-thread on a quiet champion.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain and AI-savings themes articulated (ANA 3-4% incremental savings at Tata Play, FTE bandwidth), but no Allstate-specific savings, cycle-time or TCO-vs-Ariba figure quantified.
